Review conducted read-only via Read/Grep/Glob; no Bash, edits, writes, or connector calls. VERDICT: PASS with no findings.

Production-ready claim is correctly conservative: the pack does NOT assert production_ready=true. command-evidence.json and manifest.json both record production_ready=false with status "implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout", no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false. The derived production_ready_candidate=true flag is strictly coverage.valid && foundation.valid (validators.js:1073) and is kept distinct from the pending final-closeout state — no over-claim.

Authority boundary is enforced, not merely asserted: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, and human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true are set across all five descriptors (service.js) and independently enforced per-pack by validators.js ("Claude authority boundary drift" errors at lines 205/420/616/827/1036) and by scripts/validate-rp09-crm-core-contract.mjs (promotes_claude_to_final_approver=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false). Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer; the review packet sets read_only=true.

Hermes usage is control-plane/descriptor-only: every Hermes evidence packet sets emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false (service.js:46/119/215/366/495/624/672, registry.js:118), enforced in validators.js:194/1007. No Hermes runtime is embedded in the product; gate H09 passed via deterministic contract/spec validation only.

No-write / no-real-data boundaries hold: freezeCp303Result sets all no-write/no-runtime flags false and no_real_data/synthetic_only/customer_safe_errors_only true, with extensive no-leak guards (permission_decision_detail_excluded, audit_hint_detail_excluded, no_unauthorized_count_leak, unauthorized_data_omitted, tenant/lock boundaries) validated in validators.js and spot-checked by tests (model.test.js CP303 cases). All distribution counts (deliverable, phase 13/112/25, micro-title summing to 150, 17 sections) reconcile across prompt baseline, registry, contract, plan, and tests.

CP178+ review-hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded (readiness-report.json exit_code 0, stdout_bytes 1456 — not 0-byte, valid JSON {"ok":true}), so this is valid review evidence, not a rejectable auth-failure/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped/missing-field case. The review-schema is present and enforced. All 21 pre-review validation commands (node --check x5, node --test, rp05-rp09/closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/rp00/goal/fullplan validates, npm test/validate/build, git diff --check) returned exit_code 0.

Scope discipline confirmed: the 8 active CRM implementation refs are the only pack content; unrelated dirty/untracked files (the .docx, new-session-handoff.md, docs/closeout-packs/cp00-303/, c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly preserved as out-of-scope and not treated as pack deliverables (changed-file-scope.json; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty).
